user_query:
I’m setting up a tiny local CI workflow for my underwater drone project and need a few artifacts generated from the files in input/. Please do the following using only the provided files and save outputs to the specified paths:

1) CI summary (reports/ci_summary.json):
- Read input/test_results.json and input/coverage.html.
- Write a JSON object with fields: total_tests (int), passed (int), failed (int), overall_coverage_percent (int), top_5_slowest (array of objects with name, module, duration_ms, status).
- top_5_slowest must be the five tests with the highest duration_ms, sorted descending.
- overall_coverage_percent must be the integer overall coverage extracted from input/coverage.html (do not include the % sign).

2) Slow tests CSV (reports/slow_tests.csv):
- From input/test_results.json, write the same five slowest tests into a CSV with header: name,module,duration_ms,status.
- Sort rows by duration_ms descending.

3) Coverage summary CSV (reports/coverage_summary.csv):
- Parse input/coverage.html and extract the per-file coverage table (file, lines_covered, lines_total, coverage_percent as an integer without the % sign).
- Preserve the row order from the HTML.

4) Rewrite build notifications (out/notifications_cleaned.txt):
- Read input/notifications.md. Rewrite each listed message to be concise, neutral, and in imperative mood; maximum 120 characters per message; remove emoji; keep any numeric values unchanged; avoid hedging.
- Output one rewritten message per line in the same order.

5) Generate pipeline from template (.ci/pipeline.yml):
- Read input/pipeline_template.yml, input/build_matrix.yaml, and input/coverage.html.
- Replace the placeholder {{MIN_COVERAGE}} with the integer overall coverage percent extracted from input/coverage.html (no % sign).
- Replace the placeholder {{TARGETS_INLINE}} with an inline YAML list built from the targets array in input/build_matrix.yaml, preserving the original order, e.g., [a,b,c].
- Do not modify any other parts of the template.

All outputs must be consistent with the provided inputs (e.g., counts and coverage values must match what you parse). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
